v1.letter_excerpt: The air ambulance was medically necessary given the emergency situation. Prior authorization was not possible in this emergency, and we ask that the denial be reconsidered...

v3.letter_excerpt: Ground transport was not a clinically viable option: mountain weather extended ground transit to approximately 90 minutes during an active myocardial infarction. Cigna's own guidelines cover air transport when ground transport time would endanger the patient's life — that threshold was met. The prior authorization exception for genuine emergencies applies directly...

what_changed: Earlier drafts invoked the emergency exception without providing the specific ground transport time estimate that makes the exception applicable under Cigna's own standard.
The improved version supplies the 90-minute estimate and ties it directly to the policy language, making the exception argument concrete rather than general.
